channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Charity Jonathan emphasizes workforce as key to sustainable banking success
item.description: Charity Jonathan, the Head HR Business Partner for Retail Banking at CRDB Bank, stresses that real success in banking is not only about profits but also about people. With 19 years in the industry, sh...

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal Prepared for Severe Thunderstorms and Potential Flooding on 6 March 2026
item.description: Disaster management authorities across KwaZulu-Natal (KZN) have been placed on high alert following a Level Four warning for severe thunderstorms expected to hit large parts of the province on 6 March...

item.title: Industrialists Urge Govt to Review Gas Suspension Amid Rising Power Tariffs
item.description: KARACHI: Industrialists and business leaders in Pakistan have expressed strong concern over the government’s recent decision to suspend gas supply to industries for two days per week, including curtai...

item.title: Tanzania promotes electric vehicles as fuel prices rise
item.description: As fuel prices continue to rise globally due to the Middle East conflict, Tanzania is looking at electric vehicles (EVs) as a way to ease the cost of living for its citizens. President Samia Suluhu Ha...

item.title: Western University partners with public and private sectors to expand HIV treatment access worldwide
item.description: Western University in London, Ontario, has launched an initiative combining public and private resources to address inequalities in HIV treatment access across the globe. The partnership aims to devel...

item.title: Drug dealer pleads guilty to manslaughter of vulnerable man in London cuckooing case
item.description: A 33-year-old drug dealer named Bamidele Fawehinmi has pleaded guilty at the Old Bailey to the manslaughter and causing grievous bodily harm of Dimitrios Tsavdaris, a 55-year-old vulnerable former che...

item.title: LPG Prices Hiked in India Amid Geopolitical Tensions
item.description: The Indian government has announced a significant increase in the prices of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) with effect from today. Domestic LPG cylinders will now cost Rs 60 more than the previous rate...

item.title: Understanding India's Internet Culture Through Caste and Class Lenses
item.description: Anurag Minus Verma’s book, 'The Great Indian Brain Rot', presents a unique lens on digital India, combining personal experience with social commentary. The work is a collection of nine essays explorin...

item.title: BBC apologizes for airing racial slur during BAFTAs ceremony
item.description: The BBC has expressed profound regret over a serious broadcasting error during the BAFTAs on February 22, where a racial slur was aired live as Michael B. Jordan and Delroy Lindo presented an award. T...

item.title: Welsh journalist's home searched by police in Chinese spying investigation; he was not arrested
item.description: A veteran Welsh journalist, Martin Shipton, associate editor of Nation.Cymru, has described a distressing experience after Metropolitan Police officers, supported by Welsh counter-terrorism units, for...

item.title: President Samia Suluhu Hassan Leads Major Progress in Tanzania's Sports Sector and AFCON 2027 Preparations
item.description: Tanzania iko kwenye mabadiliko makubwa katika sekta ya michezo chini ya uongozi wa Rais Samia Suluhu Hassan. Serikali imeweka nguvu kubwa katika uwekezaji wa miundombinu, sera na motisha kwa wanariadh...

item.title: Indonesia to Restrict Social Media Access for Children Under 16
item.description: Indonesia, the world's fourth most populous country, will implement a ban on social media access for children under 16, starting March 28, 2026. The decision targets 'high-risk platforms' including Ti...

item.title: Mass Displacement in Beirut as Israeli Airstrikes Target Southern Suburbs
item.description: Beirut is witnessing one of its largest mass displacements in recent history as Israeli airstrikes hit the southern suburbs and border villages. Over 500,000 residents have been forced from their home...

item.title: Renewed Efforts Needed to Protect Karura Forest
item.description: Karura Forest in Nairobi has been a symbol of environmental activism since the days of Prof Wangari Maathai, who led brave Kenyans to stop its grab in the late 1990s. Today, we face similar threats as...

item.title: Panthers dominate Broncos in 26-0 victory as errors cost Brisbane
item.description: In a one-sided NRL clash at Suncorp Stadium, the Penrith Panthers delivered a commanding 26-0 win over the Brisbane Broncos. Dylan Edwards shone with 230 running metres, six tackle busts, and a try, w...

item.title: NBI issues subpoenas to ex-Marines and officials linked to alleged cash deliveries
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has formally issued subpoenas to former lawmaker Mike Defensor, lawyer Levito Baligod, and 18 men, mostly ex-Marines, who alleged they delivered suitcases of...

item.title: 13 Repatriation Flights Bring Stranded Indian Travellers from Gulf to Mumbai Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: On Friday, a total of 13 repatriation flights landed at Mumbai airport to bring back Indian travellers stranded in the Gulf due to ongoing tensions in the Middle East. Flights operated by SpiceJet, Em...

item.title: Multilateral Wheeling as a Pathway to Expanded Market Access for South African IPPs
item.description: South Africa’s electricity landscape is evolving rapidly, not just in terms of generation, but in how power is traded. Regulatory reforms have opened the national grid to private participation, signal...

item.title: Traditional Cowboy Sport Thrives in Venezuela
item.description: In Venezuela, a traditional sport known as coleo remains a popular and culturally significant activity. Coleo involves cowboys, called llaneros, chasing cattle on horseback and attempting to topple th...

item.title: The Wings Schools in Iwo Organise Farewell Ceremony for Pioneer Provost of Federal College of Education, Iwo
item.description: The management of The Wings Schools, Iwo, in Osun State, recently held a heartfelt send-off ceremony for Professor Abdulrafiu Adebayo, who has just completed his four-year tenure as the pioneer provos...
